From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Live & Sweaty](/w/index.php?title=Live_%26_Sweaty&redirect=no "Live & Sweaty"))

1991 Australian TV series or program

| *Live and Sweaty* | |
| --- | --- |
| Genre | [Panel show](/wiki/Panel_show "Panel show") |
| Created by | [Andrew Denton](/wiki/Andrew_Denton "Andrew Denton") |
| Presented by | [Andrew Denton](/wiki/Andrew_Denton "Andrew Denton") (1991–93) [Elle McFeast](/wiki/Elle_McFeast "Elle McFeast") (1994) |
| Country of origin | Australia |
| Original language | English |
| No. of seasons | 4 |
| Original release | |
| Network | [ABC Television](/wiki/ABC_Television_(Australian_TV_network) "ABC Television (Australian TV network)") |
| Release | 22 March 1991 (1991-03-22) – 14 December 1994 (1994-12-14) |

***Live and Sweaty*** was an Australian [sports](/wiki/Sport "Sport") [television program](/wiki/Television_program "Television program"), broadcast on the [ABC](/wiki/Australian_Broadcasting_Corporation "Australian Broadcasting Corporation") from 1991 until 1994. Hosted by [Andrew Denton](/wiki/Andrew_Denton "Andrew Denton") (and later [Elle McFeast](/wiki/Elle_McFeast "Elle McFeast")), the show was part panel-based, part [talk show](/wiki/Talk_show "Talk show") and part [comedy](/wiki/Comedy "Comedy"), and predated *[The Footy Show](/wiki/The_Footy_Show_(rugby_league) "The Footy Show (rugby league)")* which has a similar format.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## *Live and Sweaty* regulars

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Live_and_Sweaty&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Live and Sweaty regulars")]

[Andrew Denton](/wiki/Andrew_Denton "Andrew Denton") was the show's host, and this was Denton's third series for the ABC (after *[Blah Blah Blah](/wiki/Blah_Blah_Blah_(TV_series) "Blah Blah Blah (TV series)")* and *[The Money or the Gun](/wiki/The_Money_or_the_Gun "The Money or the Gun")*). Comedian [Libbi Gorr](/wiki/Libbi_Gorr "Libbi Gorr") (in her [Elle McFeast](/wiki/Elle_McFeast "Elle McFeast") persona) was a roving reporter, panellist and eventual host of the show when Denton left in 1993.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Other regular panel members were actor [Lex "The Swine" Marinos](/wiki/Lex_Marinos "Lex Marinos") (always introduced with a *[Zorba the Greek](/wiki/Zorba_the_Greek "Zorba the Greek")* style musical theme), [Triple J](/wiki/Triple_J "Triple J") presenter [Debbie "Skull of Rust" Spillane](/wiki/Debbie_Spillane "Debbie Spillane"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) former [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") player [Rex "The Moose" Mossop](/wiki/Rex_Mossop "Rex Mossop"), and [Peter "Crackers" Keenan](/wiki/Peter_Keenan "Peter Keenan").[[4]](#cite_note-4) A sports news segment was presented by ABC sportscaster [Karen Tighe](/wiki/Karen_Tighe "Karen Tighe").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Notable stunts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Live_and_Sweaty&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Notable stunts")]

In 1992, Denton challenged the [Prime Minister of Australia](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Australia "Prime Minister of Australia"), [Paul Keating](/wiki/Paul_Keating "Paul Keating"), to a game of [ten-pin bowling](/wiki/Ten-pin_bowling "Ten-pin bowling"). Keating made no comment, but Denton continued to up the ante over the course of the year, protesting outside [Parliament House](/wiki/Parliament_House,_Canberra "Parliament House, Canberra") and organising petitions urging the Prime Minister to accept the challenge. Keating eventually relented, not to the game, but appeared in a live interview with Denton.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In the lead up to the [1992 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1992_Summer_Olympics "1992 Summer Olympics"), Denton and the rest of the regular cast of the show recorded the song "[I Don't Care As Long As We Beat New Zealand](/wiki/I_Don%27t_Care_As_Long_As_We_Beat_New_Zealand "I Don't Care As Long As We Beat New Zealand")" parodying the sporting rivalry between [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") and [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand").[[7]](#cite_note-7) It was released as a single and reached #38 on the Australian charts.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Live_and_Sweaty&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Discography")]

### Singles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Live_and_Sweaty&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Singles")]

| Title | Year | Peak chart positions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [AUS](/wiki/ARIA_Charts "ARIA Charts") [[10]](#cite_note-aus-10) |
| "[I Don't Care As Long As We Beat New Zealand](/wiki/I_Don%27t_Care_As_Long_As_We_Beat_New_Zealand "I Don't Care As Long As We Beat New Zealand")"   (with Andrew Denton) | 1992 | 38 |

## Awards and nominations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Live_and_Sweaty&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Awards and nominations")]

### ARIA Music Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Live_and_Sweaty&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: ARIA Music Awards")]

The [ARIA Music Awards](/wiki/ARIA_Music_Awards "ARIA Music Awards") are a set of annual ceremonies presented by [Australian Recording Industry Association](/wiki/Australian_Recording_Industry_Association "Australian Recording Industry Association") (ARIA), which recognise excellence, innovation, and achievement across all genres of the [music of Australia](/wiki/Music_of_Australia "Music of Australia"). They commenced in 1987.

| Year | Nominee / work | Award | Result | Ref. |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [1993](/wiki/ARIA_Music_Awards_of_1993 "ARIA Music Awards of 1993") | "I Don't Care As Long As We Beat New Zealand" | [Best Comedy Release](/wiki/ARIA_Award_for_Best_Comedy_Release "ARIA Award for Best Comedy Release") | Nominated | [[11]](#cite_note-awards-11) |
